title: Electrostatic discharge protection system for window coverings
abstract: An electrostatic discharge (ESD) circuit used with motorized window shades includes an antenna connected to a motor controller having a printed circuit board, a receiver or transceiver, a microprocessor and memory that are connected to and operate a motor. The ESD circuit is connected to the line-in between the antenna and the downstream components of the printed circuit board. The ESD circuit includes an inductor in parallel with two anti-parallel diodes in reverse polarity to one another. The ESD circuit shunts damaging signals or ESD events to ground by providing a lower impedance or lower resistance path to ground for these signals, whereas the ESD circuit allows the signals of interest to pass by the ESD circuit as the ESD circuit provides a higher impedance or higher resistance path to ground as compared to the signal of interest passing through the receiver or transceiver of the motor controller.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A motorized window covering, comprising: a motor; a power source operably connected to the motor; shade material operably connected to the motor such that the shade material is opened and closed by operation of the motor; a motor controller operably connected to the motor; an antenna connected to the motor controller by a line-in; an ESD circuit connected to the line-in; and wherein the ESD circuit shunts ESD events to ground thereby preventing ESD events from reaching downstream components of the motor controller. 2. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein an ESD event is a high voltage low current transient event. 3. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein an ESD event is a transient event above a predetermined voltage. 4. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein an ESD event is a transient event below a predetermined voltage. 5. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein an ESD event is a transient event above or below a predetermined voltage. 6. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the ESD circuit includes two anti- parallel diodes positioned in reverse polarities to one another. 7. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the ESD circuit includes an inductor. 8. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the ESD circuit includes an inductor positioned in parallel with two anti -parallel diodes positioned in reverse polarities to one another. 9. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the ESD circuit creates a path of lower impedance to ground for ESD events while creating a path of higher impedance to ground for signals of interest. 10. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the ESD circuit creates a path of lower resistance to ground for ESD events while creating a path of higher resistance to ground for signals of interest. 1 1. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the line-in connects to a receiver or transceiver. 12. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the motor controller includes a receiver or transceiver, a microprocessor and memory. 13. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the shade material is connected to a rotatable tube wherein the motor and power source are positioned within the rotatable tube. 14. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the antenna is selected from the group consisting of a monopole antenna, a loop antenna, a meandering monopole antenna, a fractal antenna and a slot antenna. 15. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the power source is a plurality of batteries. 16. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the ESD circuit is tuned to allow passage of signals of interest on a predetermined specific range of frequencies. 17. The motorized window r covering of claim 1 further comprising a remote wirelessly connected to the antenna wherein the remote is programmed to transmit signals of interest on a predetermined specific range of frequencies. 18. The motorized window covering of claim 1 wherein the shade material generates an ESD event. 19. A motorized window covering, comprising: a motor; a power source operably connected to the motor; shade material operably connected to the motor such that the shade material is opened and closed by the motor; a motor controller operably connected to the motor; an antemia connected to the motor controller by a line-in; an ESD circuit operatively connected to the line-in, the ESD circuit having an inductor in parallel with two anti-parallel diodes; wherein the ESD circuit shunts ESD events to ground thereby preventing ESD events from reaching downstream components of the motor controller. 20. The motorized window covering of claim 19 witerein the two anti-parallel diodes are positioned in reverse polarities to one another. 21. The motorized window covering of claim 19 wherein the ESD circuit creates a path of lower impedance to ground for ESD events while creating a path of higher impedance to ground for signals of interest. 22. The motorized window r covering of claim 19 wherein the ESD circuit creates a path of lower resistance to ground for ESD events while creating a path of higher resistance to ground for signals of interest. 23. The motorized window covering of claim 19 wherein the line-in connects to a receiver or transceiver. 24. The motorized window covering of claim 19 witerein the power source is a plurality of batteries. 25. The motorized window coverings of claim 19 wherein the ESD circuit is tuned to allow passage of signals of interest on a specific range of frequencies. 26. The motorized window coverings of claim 14 further comprising a remote wirelessly connected to the antenna wherein the remote is programmed to transmit signals of interest on a specific range of frequencies. 27. A motorized window covering, comprising: a motor; a power source operably connected to the motor; shade material operably connected to the motor such that the shade material is opened and closed by operation of the motor; a motor controller operably connected to the motor; the motor controller having a receiver or transceiver; an antenna connected to the receiver or transceiver by a line-in; an ESD circuit connected to the line-in; the ESD circuit having an inductor in parallel with two anti-parallel diodes, witerein the anti- parallel diodes are positioned in reverse polarity to one another; wherein the ESD circuit creates a path of lower impedance to ground or lower resistance to ground for ESD events thereby preventing ESD events from reaching the receiver or transceiver. 28. The motorized window covering of claim 27 wherein the ESD circuit creates a path of higher impedance to ground or higher resistance to ground for signals of interest. 29. A method of protecting a motorized window covering from an ESD event, comprising the steps of: providing a motorized window r covering having motor and a motor controller having a receiver or transceiver; providing an antenna connected to the receiver or transceiver by a line-in; providing an ESD circuit on or in the line-in; creating a path of lower impedance to ground or lower resistance to ground for ESD events by the ESD circuit; creating a path of higher impedance to ground or higher resistance to ground for signals of interest by the ESD circuit. 30. The method of protecting a motorized window covering from an ESD event of claim 29, wherein the ESD circuit includes an inductor in parallel with two anti-parallel diodes, wherein the anti-parallel diodes are positioned in reverse polarity to one another.
